methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

private.name: Shinkei
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Daylight Computer
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Whisper Aero
private.rationale: Same sector (Consumer Tech), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Framework Computer, a Series A consumer tech company founded by Nirav Patel, is evaluated against a private comparables universe of three peers: Shinkei, Daylight Computer, and Whisper Aero. No public trading comps are available in this nascent frontier-tech segment.

The valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using Framework's $35M in total raised as a baseline value proxy, a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $70M. This progression methodology is grounded in the company's capital-raise history rather than revenue multiples or DCF inputs, reflecting the early-stage, pre-scale nature of the business.

The triangulated range spans $60M (low) to $80.5M (high), with a median of $70M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single valuation methodology. Key uncertainties center on the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific sector and company profile, the limited comp dataset, and the absence of unit economics or forward-revenue visibility needed to validate stage-progression assumptions. Additional revenue or profitability data, and expanded private comparable set depth, would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
